reason: Failed publishing gate: every load-bearing claim (production timeline, Broadcom/TSMC partnership, 7GW->14GW capacity target) traces to a single origin -- a Reuters exclusive citing an internal Meta memo -- which all press coverage (TechCrunch, CNBC, Yahoo Finance/Reuters wire, US News) re-reports without independent confirmation. Meta declined to comment when asked, so no Tier-0 confirmation exists either. Per policy/verification.md, business claims need >=2 independent Tier<=2 sources or a filing/document; this does not clear that bar.
watch: A Meta statement or filing confirming/denying the chip production timeline
A second, independently-sourced report (not citing the same Reuters memo) with corroborating or conflicting details
September 2026 itself, when production would start if the report is accurate -- can be checked directly
cycle_2026-07-10_c1_note: Re-checked (2 web searches). All new coverage (Yahoo, CNBC, US News, thenextweb, MLQ, androidheadlines, euronext) still traces to the same Reuters/memo exclusive -- no new independent source. One promising lead: TrendForce's 2026-01-30 MTIA-3 article predates the Reuters memo and cites Commercial Times (supply-chain sources) independently -- but it covers a different claim set (MTIA-3, TSMC 3nm, 2H26 debut) than the memo's claims (chip 'Iris', September start, 7GW->14GW capacity, Broadcom partnership through 2029). Did not have budget this cycle to fully reconcile whether MTIA-3/Iris are the same chip or resolve the node/date discrepancy -- needs a dedicated check, not a rushed one. Still watching.
cycle_2026-07-10_c2_note: Re-checked (1 web search + 1 live fetch). New coverage found (unboxfuture.com, cryptobriefing, qz.com, kucoin) all still trace to the same internal-memo/Reuters origin -- fetched unboxfuture.com directly and confirmed it cites only an unnamed internal Meta memo and an unnamed 'Infrastructure Operations Lead' quote, no independent verification. No Meta statement/filing has surfaced. Did not have remaining cycle budget to reconcile the MTIA-3/Iris lead from cycle 1. Still watching -- next check point is September 2026 itself (production start date), or an independent second source.
cycle_2026-07-10_night_c1_note: Reconciled the MTIA-3/Iris lead (2 web searches + 1 live fetch). RESOLVED: MTIA-3 and 'Iris' are the same chip generation (Meta's third custom AI training/inference chip) -- TrendForce's TSMC 3nm claim matches the process node reported for Iris, and Electronics Weekly's 'Meta prepares to fab its third custom datacentre chip' framing corroborates the MTIA-3=Iris identification. This gives TrendForce (independent, Commercial Times supply-chain sourcing) as a second, genuinely independent source for the narrower claim 'Meta's next MTIA chip (MTIA-3/Iris) enters production on TSMC 3nm in 2H26' -- that narrower claim now clears the >=2-independent-source bar. However the full bundled claim set this entry was opened for -- Iris codename, September 2026 start specifically, 7GW->14GW capacity target, Broadcom partnership through 2029 -- still traces to ONE origin (the Reuters memo) for its most newsworthy elements (capacity, Broadcom deal terms); TrendForce does not corroborate those. Also checked Meta's own official blog (about.fb.com/news/2026/03, custom-silicon post) as a Tier-0 candidate -- it discusses MTIA 300/400/450/500 and a Broadcom collaboration in passing but does not mention Iris, MTIA-3, a September date, or the 7GW->14GW figures, so it does not confirm the gated set either. Gate still does NOT pass for the story as scoped. Flagging for the owner/next cycle: a narrower, now-corroborated story ('Meta confirms next custom AI chip on TSMC 3nm for late 2026') could be a smaller publishable unit if editorially desired -- would need its own fresh verify+adversarial pass scoped to only the TrendForce-corroborated claims (chip generation, node, half-year timing), explicitly excluding the capacity and Broadcom-2029 figures. Still watching.
cycle_2026-07-10_night_c2_note: Quick re-check (1 web search, ~30min window this cycle). New outlets surfaced (yournews.com, threads.com repost, finance.yahoo.com) but all still re-report the same internal-memo/Reuters origin -- no new independent Tier<=2 source, no Meta statement or filing. Gate still fails on the independence rule for the bundled claim set. Still watching; the narrower TrendForce-corroborated sub-story flagged in cycle 1 remains unactioned.
cycle_2026-07-10_night_c3_note: Re-checked (2 web searches). Still no new independent corroboration -- all coverage (Yahoo, letsdatascience, MLQ, Android Headlines, cryptobriefing, qz.com, US News, ITP.net, X/Threads reposts) traces to the single 9 July Reuters exclusive. Meta still declined to comment. Found one older, genuinely independent data point predating the memo by ~3 months: CNBC, 2026-04-14, 'Meta commits to 1 gigawatt of custom chips with Broadcom as Hock Tan decides to leave board' -- independent by timing, but only corroborates a general Meta-Broadcom chip relationship at a different scale (1GW), not the bundled claims this entry covers (Iris name, September start, 7GW->14GW target, 2029 term). Gate still fails. Still watching; TrendForce-corroborated narrower sub-story from cycle 1 remains unactioned and available if the owner wants it split out.

Same result as every prior recheck tonight: all coverage (Threads/X reposts, Yahoo Finance, letsdatascience, cryptobriefing, Android Headlines, MLQ, US News) still traces to the single 9 July Reuters exclusive/internal memo. No Meta statement, no filing, no new independent Tier<=2 source. This is the fifth recheck across tonight's two invocations with an unchanged result -- diminishing returns on further per-cycle rechecks until either September 2026 (claimed production start) or a genuinely new source arrives. Still watching.
